The Vault v0.3.0 adds Graphify as an optional project graph extension for local code intelligence.

Highlights:
- Adds a managed Graphify runtime surface in Settings for detecting, installing, and configuring Graphify without vendoring it into The Vault.
- Adds per-project Graphify source-root mapping, build status, artifact discovery, graph freshness, and report/HTML fallback handling.
- Embeds Graphify's generated `graph.html` in the desktop Graph view when available, with `graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md` fallbacks.
- Adds Vault MCP Graphify tools for status, graph queries, node lookup, neighbors, shortest path, impact explanation, graph builds, and recall with graph context.
- Adds graph-aware recall that combines Vault memory with likely relevant files, tests, paths, communities, and suggested next reads.
- Adds instruction sync so Codex and Claude setup prompts can learn when to use the new Graphify tools.
- Adds token-savings telemetry and Activity tracking for Graphify-assisted recall and MCP tool calls.
- Keeps Graphify optional and failure-isolated; memory and MCP connector flows continue to work without Graphify installed.
- Hardens Windows Graphify staging by excluding packaged/build artifacts, sidecar OS files, and read-only `desktop.ini` paths that caused EPERM rebuild failures.
- Improves Graphify query ranking/path normalization and compacts MCP graph responses to keep agent context small.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, restart The Vault and reconnect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code so the updated MCP tools are loaded.
